title: Export Repository Objects

Export Repository Objects

Export Repository Objects

Use this form to Export Repository definitions for selected objects to an XML export file.

The exported definitions can then be imported into a compatible Repository on the same platform, or on a different platform. For more information, see Export and Import Facilities.

How to

  1. Use a retrieve profile to select the objects to export. You can do any of the following:
    • Click Browse to browse for and select an object.
    • Enter a GOLD ; separated list of component names; for example, ORDER_ENTRY;ORDER_LIST
    • Enter a retrieve profile using GOLD * ; for example ORDER_*
    • Click Select All to enter the wildcard GOLD * in every field, thereby exporting the entire Repository
  2. Enter a file name or choose an existing file, and select Append if you want to add to an existing file. If this check box is cleared, the existing file is overwritten.
  3. Click Export to export the selected definitions.

Fields

Use the fields in this form to select or enter the names of objects to be exported. Note the following:

  • When you choose to export Uniface Components or Signatures, you can use Component Options to specify that related objects should also be exported.
    • Include signatures—include the signatures of the specified components
    • Include Uniface Components—include the Uniface component implementations described by the signatures.
    • Include Component name Include ProcScript—also export Include ProcScript stored in a library that has the same name as the component.
    • Scope—restrict the components to a particular type, or include or exclude the modeled components on which the components are based.
  • Modeled properties implicitly belong to typed libraries called LIB. You can export modeled properties of a specific type by name.
  • For Entities and DTDs, you can restrict the export to modeled entities and DTDs that have the same value for their Model Name property.
  • To export untyped libraries, you must specify the names in the Libraries field and the specific objects you want to export. Use GOLD * to select all objects.

Buttons

  • Browse—browse for objects in the current field.
  • Select All—export all Repository objects. This automatically enters GOLD * (the profile character to retrieve all) in every field on the form.
  • Export—export the specified Repository objects to a file.
  • Close—close the form.
Related Links
  • Export Development Objects

    You can export application definitions from the Repository to an XML file. This is useful when you need to share objects with a colleague, or backup a set of objects.
